skill.name: ai-chapter-consolidate
skill.description: Use AI to merge individual page HTML files into a unified chapter document. Creates continuous document format for improved reading experience and semantic consistency.
skill.summary: Use AI to merge individual page HTML files into a unified chapter document. Creates continuous docum...
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but most are Markdown examples or ordinary documentation text. The meaningful risks are bounded filesystem reads and writes, an optional local Python wrapper, and sending full page content to an AI model. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, obfuscation, or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Read and Write Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to load chapter page HTML files and save a consolidated HTML document and log. This is expected behavior, but it should be limited to the intended project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is explicit and central to the skill. It appears legitimate, but filesystem access still requires workspace boundaries.

medium_findings.title: Optional Local Python Wrapper Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents running a local Python wrapper with chapter, page range, output, and mapping arguments. The command appears legitimate, but the referenced script is outside this one-file skill and was not reviewed here.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is shown in a bash example and uses fixed flags. Risk remains because executing an unreviewed local script depends on the surrounding repository.

medium_findings.title: Full Document Content Sent to AI Model
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends all page contents to Claude for consolidation. This may expose sensitive or licensed source material to the AI service used by the operator.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to send all page contents to Claude is explicit. The impact depends on the operator's data policy and document sensitivity.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misclassified as Commands
low_findings.description: Most static command findings are inline filenames, HTML tags, fenced prompt examples, or fenced output examples. These are documentation examples, not direct shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly Markdown documentation or HTML output examples. No executable interpolation or user-controlled shell construction appears in those locations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Relative Asset Paths
low_findings.description: The ../../ entries are stylesheet links in HTML examples and relative arguments for the documented wrapper. They are not shown as user-controlled traversal into arbitrary files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are fixed examples inside documentation and use normal project-relative references. I did not find evidence of arbitrary path input or traversal logic.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak crypto alerts point to descriptive AI-related text, not hashing or encryption. The reconnaissance alerts are normal validation tasks such as checking file existence and HTML parseability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural language about AI and validation. No cryptographic algorithm, system probing, or host reconnaissance code was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Local Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The only confirmed command pattern is a documented Python wrapper call. It is not malicious by itself, but users should review the local script before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is concrete and executable, but it is presented as intended workflow documentation. No injection pattern is visible in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Teams often need to combine page-level HTML into one readable chapter without losing structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through extraction, consolidation, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Collects numbered per-page HTML files for one chapter.
Extracts main page content while preserving semantic classes.
Prompts an AI model to merge pages into one HTML5 chapter.
Removes duplicate chapter headers, navigation, and page breaks.
Checks consolidated output for structure, headings, and completeness.
Creates a consolidation log with page and content statistics.
limitations: It depends on an external consolidation script or orchestrator.
It requires valid per-page HTML from earlier pipeline steps.
AI output needs validation before publication.
Sensitive source documents may be sent to the AI model.

use_cases.title: Merge Training Manual Chapters
use_cases.description: Combine many page exports into one continuous chapter for review and publishing.

use_cases.title: Normalize HTML Structure
use_cases.description: Preserve semantic classes while removing repeated headers, navigation, and page markers.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI-Assisted Publishing Output
use_cases.description: Generate a single chapter file and quality checklist for downstream validation gates.

prompt_templates.title: Consolidate One Chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard chapter merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to consolidate chapter 2 from the available per-page HTML files. Preserve all text and semantic classes.

prompt_templates.title: Merge With Validation Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that asks for review findings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Consolidate chapter 4 and report any missing pages, duplicate sections, or heading hierarchy concerns before saving the final output.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Complex Elements
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for tables, images, and exhibits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge chapter 7 while preserving all figures, tables, captions, image paths, and exhibit labels. Flag any element that cannot be verified.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Consolidation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for checking a generated chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the consolidated chapter file against the source page files. Confirm content coverage, heading order, duplicate removal, and log accuracy.

output_examples.input: A chapter split across fourteen page-level HTML files with repeated headers.
output_examples.output: A single continuous chapter document with one chapter header, one content area, preserved sections, and no repeated page navigation.

output_examples.input: A merged chapter with possible duplicated continuation headings.
output_examples.output: A validation summary that identifies duplicate headings, confirms preserved paragraphs, and lists corrections needed before publication.

output_examples.input: Per-page files containing tables, captions, and image references.
output_examples.output: A consolidated chapter that keeps tables, captions, image references, and semantic classes in the reading order.

best_practices: Run the skill only inside the intended project workspace.
Review source page counts before consolidation.
Validate the merged chapter before publishing or archiving it.
anti_patterns: Do not run the wrapper script before reviewing local repository code.
Do not consolidate sensitive documents without an approved AI data policy.
Do not publish AI-generated HTML without a structural validation pass.

faq.question: Does this skill only concatenate files?
faq.answer: No. It guides AI-assisted merging that removes repeated page elements and normalizes structure.

faq.question: What input format does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects validated per-page HTML files with a main page-content area.

faq.question: Can it preserve images and tables?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires preserving figures, tables, captions, image paths, and semantic classes.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: The skill documents an optional Python wrapper command. Operators should review the script before running it.

faq.question: Is the output ready to publish?
faq.answer: Not by itself. The consolidated chapter should pass semantic and visual validation gates first.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
